shlokas.sanskrit:
सर्वे भवन्तु सुखिनः। सर्वे सन्तु निरामयाः।
सर्वे भद्राणि पश्यन्तु। मा कश्चिद् दुःखभाग् भवेत्॥
shlokas.meaning: May all be happy. May all be free from illness. May all see auspiciousness. May none suffer.

shlokas.sanskrit:
असतो मा सद्गमय। तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय।
मृत्योर्मा अमृतं गमय॥
shlokas.meaning: Lead me from the unreal to the real, from darkness to light, from death to immortality.

shlokas.sanskrit:
वक्रतुण्ड महाकाय सूर्यकोटि समप्रभ।
निर्विघ्नं कुरु मे देव सर्वकार्येषु सर्वदा॥
shlokas.meaning: O Lord with the curved trunk and great body, radiant as a million suns — make all my undertakings free of obstacles, always.

shlokas.sanskrit:
कराग्रे वसते लक्ष्मीः करमध्ये सरस्वती।
करमूले तु गोविन्दः प्रभाते करदर्शनम्॥
shlokas.meaning: At the fingertips dwells Lakshmi, in the palm Saraswati, at the wrist Govinda — gaze at your hands at dawn.

shlokas.sanskrit:
त्वमेव माता च पिता त्वमेव।
त्वमेव बन्धुश्च सखा त्वमेव।
त्वमेव विद्या द्रविणं त्वमेव।
त्वमेव सर्वं मम देव देव॥
shlokas.meaning: You alone are mother and father, kin and friend, knowledge and wealth — You are everything to me, O God of Gods.

shlokas.sanskrit:
ॐ त्र्यम्बकं यजामहे सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धनम्।
उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान्मृत्योर्मुक्षीय मामृतात्॥
shlokas.meaning: We worship the three-eyed One, the fragrant nourisher. As the cucumber is freed from its stem, so free us from death — but not from immortality.

shlokas.sanskrit:
सह नाववतु। सह नौ भुनक्तु।
सह वीर्यं करवावहै। तेजस्वि नावधीतमस्तु।
मा विद्विषावहै॥
shlokas.meaning: May we be protected together, nourished together, work together with vigour. May our learning be brilliant; may we never quarrel.
May all the worlds be happy.

shlokas.sanskrit:
या कुन्देन्दु तुषारहार धवला या शुभ्रवस्त्रावृता।
या वीणावरदण्डमण्डितकरा या श्वेतपद्मासना॥
shlokas.meaning: White as jasmine, the moon, snow — clad in pure white, holding the veena, seated on the white lotus — Goddess Saraswati, source of wisdom.

shlokas.sanskrit:
मनोजवं मारुततुल्यवेगं जितेन्द्रियं बुद्धिमतां वरिष्ठम्।
वातात्मजं वानरयूथमुख्यं श्रीरामदूतं शरणं प्रपद्ये॥
shlokas.meaning: Swift as thought and wind, master of the senses, foremost among the wise — son of the wind, chief of vanaras, messenger of Rama — I take refuge in Him.

shlokas.sanskrit:
ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः। तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं।
भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि। धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्॥
shlokas.meaning: We meditate on the radiant glory of the divine Sun — may He inspire our intellect.

shlokas.sanskrit:
यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत।
अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम्॥
shlokas.meaning: Whenever righteousness declines and unrighteousness rises, O Bharata, I manifest Myself.

shlokas.sanskrit:
कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन।
मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि॥
shlokas.meaning: You have a right to action alone, never to its fruits. Do not be motivated by the fruits, nor attached to inaction.
Peace, peace, peace — within, around, and beyond.
